commit.message:
Survey n→3 and n→4 max-volume projection symmetries

- Add survey_symmetric_projections.py: systematic volume optimization and
  symmetry computation for n→3 (n=4..10) and n→4 (n=5..10)
- Fix symmetry computation bug (R = dst.T @ inv(src.T), not dst @ inv(src))
- Key findings: 10→3 has icosahedral symmetry (order 120) — genuinely
  non-trivial. Most other non-trivial cases have low symmetry.
- Document n→k / n→(n-k) duality: cases with k∈{1,2,n-1,n-2} are
  trivially solved and their high symmetry is expected.
- Update README: add methods section, Result 12 survey table, open questions

* ci(security): add parallel security scanning workflow

Five jobs run in parallel; all upload HTML/JSON artifacts (30-day retention):

- sast: opengrep with p/security-audit + p/owasp-top-ten rules

weekly schedule (Mon 03:00 UTC), and workflow_dispatch.

* fix(ring-sig): zeroize t_i byte encoding after last use in rsig()

randomScalar() now returns { scalar: bigint, bytes: Uint8Array }.
After r[idx] is computed (the last arithmetic use of t_i.scalar),
t_i.bytes.fill(0) is called to zeroize the 57-byte LE encoding.

This eliminates the most actionable secret-scalar copy: t_i is the nonce
whose disclosure would allow recovery of the signer's private scalar a_i
via r_i = t_i - c_i * a_i.

a_i is returned by @noble/curves as a bigint and cannot be intercepted
in byte form; all bigint arithmetic intermediates similarly persist in
the GC-managed heap until collected — these are inherent JS/noble
limitations, accurately documented in the header JSDoc and
security-invariants.md (invariant #5 updated from 'accepted limitation,
revisit later' to 'partial mitigation, maximum achievable in current
stack').

All 104 tests pass.

* fix(tsconfig): replace noEmit with emitDeclarationOnly in package tsconfigs

TS6310: composite project references require emit to be enabled.
noEmit disables all emit (including .d.ts), which is incompatible with
project references. emitDeclarationOnly satisfies composite mode while
still producing only declaration files (no JS output).

commit.message:
Remove SDK session mode feature — always use server default

The SDK Mode API (Interactive/Plan/Autopilot) was making the LLM less
verbose in Autopilot mode due to server-side system prompt injection.
VS Code doesn't use this API at all — its Agent/Ask/Plan are intent
handlers, not SDK modes. Lumi should always be agentic and conversational.

- Remove mode selector from both composers in ChatView.axaml
- Remove ModeLabels, SessionMode, SelectedMode, and all mode sync logic
- Remove SetSessionModeAsync / SetSessionModeSilent / OnSessionModeChanged
- Remove per-message Mode from MessageOptions
- Remove session mode restore on chat switch and session creation
- Remove GetSessionModeAsync / SetSessionModeAsync from CopilotService
- Keep Plan panel and Plan API (server can still generate plans)
- Keep SessionMode on Chat model for backward-compatible deserialization

commit.message:
[MrBot] Make /PROFILE linker flag opt-in via use_profile cmake option (#288)

* [MrBot] Make /PROFILE linker flag opt-in via use_profile cmake option

Add a use_profile cmake option (default OFF) to guard the /PROFILE
linker flag on test targets. The /PROFILE flag enables profiling
instrumentation needed by some code coverage tools, but it can cause
severe performance degradation (13x+ slower) when combined with VSTest
code coverage collection on projects with many test DLLs. The overhead
compounds during DLL load/initialization, turning a ~3 minute test run
into a 45+ minute timeout.

Consumers that need /PROFILE for their coverage tooling can enable it
with -Duse_profile:bool=ON in their cmake configuration.

[msbuild] Fix race condition when generating MSBStrings.Designer.cs (#24826)

When the Xamarin.Localization.MSBuild project was changed to multi-target (netstandard2.0 + net10.0), both inner builds run in parallel and both try to generate MSBStrings.Designer.cs to the same path. One build may write the file while the other is compiling it, causing:

    CSC : error CS1504: Source file 'MSBStrings.Designer.cs' could not be opened -- Attempted to read past the end of the stream.

Fix by directing StronglyTypedFileName to $(IntermediateOutputPath), which is per-TFM (obj/Debug/netstandard2.0/ vs obj/Debug/net10.0/), so each inner build generates its own copy without interfering with the other.

feat(api): add artists list pagination/filter/sort contract (#9) (#169)

* feat(api): add validated artists list query contract (refs #9)

* feat(api): wire artists list/get to sqlite repository (refs #9)

* fix(api): harden artist list sort/pagination pipeline (#171)

* Initial plan

* fix(api): address review feedback on artist list pagination

- Replace `as usize` casts with safe `usize::try_from()` conversions that
  return an empty page on overflow, avoiding potential wrapping on 32-bit
  targets (offset/limit in apply_list_query)
- Replace `sort_by` with `sort_by_cached_key` so name/status sort keys are
  computed once per element rather than on every comparison, and add a UUID
  secondary sort key as a deterministic tie-breaker for stable pagination
- Add four unit tests covering each invalid-input 400 branch: negative
  offset, invalid status, invalid sort_by, invalid sort_order

Closes #169 feedback

Co-authored-by: SvetaKrava <17299577+SvetaKrava@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): sort after bounds check, non-allocating status key, tie-breaker test

- Move total/offset/limit conversion and start>=len early-return before
  the sort so out-of-range requests skip O(n log n) work entirely
- Replace a.status.to_string() in Status sort arm with a u8 rank to
  avoid heap allocation per element (Continuing=0, Ended=1)
- Add apply_query_sort_is_deterministic_when_primary_keys_equal test:
  two artists with same name, known UUIDs from_u128(1/2), submitted in
  both orders; asserts UUID tie-breaker always gives the same result
- Add uuid as dev-dependency for test

Co-authored-by: SvetaKrava <17299577+SvetaKrava@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): import ArtistId in tie-breaker test to fix E0433 compile error

The test `apply_query_sort_is_deterministic_when_primary_keys_equal` used
`ArtistId` but the test module's `use super::*` does not pull in
`chorrosion_domain::ArtistId`. Add an explicit `use chorrosion_domain::ArtistId`
inside the test function to resolve the CI failure.

Co-authored-by: SvetaKrava <17299577+SvetaKrava@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): rename ListArtistsQueryError variants to fix clippy::enum_variant_names

Clippy rejects the enum because all variants share the `Invalid` prefix.
Rename variants by removing the prefix: InvalidLimit → Limit, InvalidOffset →
Offset, InvalidStatus → Status, InvalidSortBy → SortBy, InvalidSortOrder →
SortOrder. Update all usages (Display impl, normalize_list_query, tests).
